title: NOC 72423 – Motorcycle, all-terrain vehicle and other related mechanics | GTR Immigration
description: In 2026, Motorcycle and ATV Mechanics (NOC 72423) are the specialized technicians powering Canada’s recreational and utility vehicle sectors. As the country sees a record surge in "adventure tourism" and the adoption of electric powersports, these professionals have transitioned into high-tech roles. Beyond traditional engine overhauls, they now manage electronic fuel injection (EFI) mapping, lithium-ion battery diagnostics, and complex suspension telemetry. Operating at the TEER 2 level, they are essential to a sector where 90% of workers are full-time, and a significant 18% are self-employed entrepreneurs running their own specialized shops.

classification.teer: FeatureDetailsBroad Occupational Category7 – Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupationsTEER Category2 (Occupations usually requiring college, apprenticeship, or 2+ years of experience)Major Group72 – Technical trades and transportation officersSub-Major Group724 – Machine repair and maintenance trades

duties: Digital Diagnostics: Using 2026-grade handheld diagnostic scanners and laptop software to isolate faults in engine management systems and ABS modules.
Mechanical Repair: Repairing and overhauling engines, transmissions, clutches, and fuel injection systems to exact manufacturer tolerances.
Structural Service: Repairing and replacing chassis components, including specialized frames and high-performance suspension systems.
Performance Tuning: Adjusting and "mapping" ignition and fuel systems for optimal performance and 2026-grade emission compliance.
Specialized Maintenance: Servicing and repairing industrial powered lift trucks and marine outboard motors for commercial clients.
employment_requirements: Education: Completion of secondary school is typically required.
Apprenticeship: Completion of a three- to four-year apprenticeship program OR a combination of over four years of work experience and industry courses is required for certification.
Certification: Trade certification for Motorcycle Technicians is compulsory in Ontario (NOC 310G). It is voluntary but highly recommended in all other provinces for higher wages.
Specific Endorsements: Mechanics working on powered lift trucks or marine engines may require additional provincial safety endorsements.
Red Seal: The Interprovincial Standards Red Seal is the "Gold Standard," allowing you to work in any province and access high-paying manufacturer-direct roles.
labour_market_demand: The 2026 outlook is Moderate to Good, with "High Hiring Difficulty" reported by 82% of employers in Western Canada:

Ontario: Very Good prospects. In February 2026, Ontario issued targeted OINP invitations for NOC 72423 under the Employer Job Offer: Foreign Worker stream. The minimum score required was as low as 50, reflecting a high priority for mechanics to support regional retail and repair hubs in Southwestern Ontario.

Alberta: Highest Earning Potential. Alberta reports significant hiring difficulties, with 30% of vacancies remaining unfilled for over 4 months. Median wages are the highest in Canada at $36.81/hr, with top-tier master mechanics earning up to $58.31/hr.

British Columbia: Moderate to Good. B.C. projects over 2,750 people working in this occupation through 2027. Demand is steady in the "Mainland/Southwest" and "Vancouver Island" regions for both recreational and industrial lift-truck maintenance.

National Trend: Over 90% of workers in this NOC receive comprehensive non-wage benefits. The 2026 market highly favors those who can service hybrid and electric propulsion systems in ATVs and snowmobiles.
Why Work in Canada as a Motorcycle/ATV Mechanic?
Lead the Powersports Tech: Gain experience with 2026-grade technologies, including Bosch Cornering ABS, semi-active electronic suspension, and high-voltage EV drivetrains.
Unmatched Sector Stability: Your skills are vital for diverse industries: Retail Trade (41%), specialized Repair & Maintenance (19%), and even Federal Government public admin (7%).
Path to Business Ownership: With 18% self-employment, Canada offers one of the best environments for international mechanics to eventually open their own "Custom or Performance" shops.
Priority for PR: In early 2026, this NOC remains a core target for the Express Entry "Trade Occupations" category and is a frequent feature in OINP targeted trades draws.
pathways: OINP Employer Job Offer: In February 2026, OINP issued invitations to candidates in NOC 72423 with a score of 50+ under the Foreign Worker stream. This is a prime route for those with an Ontario job offer.

2026 Express Entry (Trade Occupations Category): Updated for 2026. Eligible candidates now require 12 months of full-time experience (up from 6 months in 2025) within the last 3 years to qualify for lower CRS threshold draws.

BC PNP Skilled Worker: A reliable route for technicians with a job offer from a BC-based powersports dealership or industrial maintenance firm, particularly in the "Thompson-Okanagan" and "Vancouver Island" regions.
2026 TR to PR Pathway: A confirmed initiative targeting up to 33,000 temporary residents. Powersports mechanics are classified as "High-Priority Essential Service Workers."
